label: Azure Postgres
description: Connect to an Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server using host, port, database name, and credentials over SSL.

form.title: Connect Azure Postgres
form.subtitle: Connect Anton to your Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server instance.

methods.label: Host & credentials
methods.description: Paste your server hostname, port, database name, and credentials. SSL is enforced by Azure — no extra setup needed.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with host & credentials

Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server requires SSL. Gather the connection details from the Azure Portal before filling in the fields below.

## Steps

1. Open the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com) and navigate to **Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible servers**.
2. Select your server instance.
3. In the left menu, click **Overview**. Copy the **Server name** — it looks like `yourdb.postgres.database.azure.com`.
4. Note the **Admin username** shown on the Overview page (format: `adminuser`).
5. The port is **5432** by default. Confirm under **Connection strings** if you've changed it.
6. Click **Databases** in the left menu to find the target database name (or use `postgres` for the default).
7. If you forgot the admin password, reset it under **Settings → Reset password**.
8. Ensure SSL is enabled: go to **Settings → Server parameters**, search for `ssl_enforcement_enabled`, and confirm it is `ON` (it is enforced by default on Flexible Server).
9. If connecting from outside Azure, add your IP under **Networking → Firewall rules → Add current client IP**.
10. Paste all values into the fields below and click **Connect**.

## Notes

- The admin username typically follows the format `adminuser` (no @servername suffix — Flexible Server differs from Single Server in this regard).
- SSL mode `require` is the minimum; use `verify-full` for stricter certificate verification.
- If your server is in a VNet with no public endpoint, you'll need a private endpoint or VPN to reach it from Anton.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Server hostname
fields.description: Found on the Azure Portal → your Flexible Server → Overview → Server name.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: Default PostgreSQL port is 5432. Change only if you've configured a custom port.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database name
fields.description: The name of the database to connect to. Use 'postgres' if you haven't created a custom one yet.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: The admin or app username. On Flexible Server, do not append @servername — just the username itself.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: The password for the above user. Reset it in Azure Portal → Settings → Reset password if needed.

fields.name: ssl_mode
fields.label: SSL mode
fields.description: SSL is mandatory on Azure Flexible Server. 'require' encrypts the connection; 'verify-full' also validates the server certificate.
